techniques.evidence_quote: authenticated API calls with valid IAM user access keys
DescribeSecurityGroups was called to determine the level of application tier access... A subsequent DescribeInstances call identified available EC2 instances
The attacker then created a SSH key pair (our initial lead alert for CreateKeyPair) for an existing EC2 instance
this series of actions resulted in command line access to the targeted EC2 instance

techniques.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Firewall
techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker "backdoored" all of the security groups... This allowed for any TCP/IP access into the victim's environment.

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained and used 8 compromised IAM user access keys (suspected to have been found in a public repo or lifted from developer machines) to authenticate to AWS via the CLI from two suspicious IPs.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they had access to a total of eight different IAM user access keys, and was active from two different IPs

evidence_chunks.quote: they had access to a total of eight different IAM user access keys, and was active from two different IPs
they had access to a total of eight different IAM user access keys, and was active from two different IPs

attack_chain.description: Attacker called DescribeSecurityGroups to enumerate the victim's security group configuration and application tier access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: DescribeSecurityGroups was called to determine the level of application tier access (firewall access) into the victim's AWS environment

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Firewall
attack_chain.description: Attacker 'backdoored' all security groups (similar to aws_pwn's backdoor_all_security_group) via hundreds of AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress calls, including rules for TCP/5432 (PostgreSQL) and 1253, exposing EC2/RDS instances to the internet.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker "backdoored" all of the security groups with a utility similar to aws_pwn's backdoor_all_security_group script... 344 calls to the AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress API

attack_chain.description: Attacker called DescribeInstances to enumerate available EC2 instances accessible to the compromised IAM user.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A subsequent DescribeInstances call identified available EC2 instances to the IAM user

evidence_chunks.quote: A subsequent DescribeInstances call identified available EC2 instances to the IAM user
A subsequent DescribeInstances call identified available EC2 instances to the IAM user

attack_chain.description: Attacker used CreateKeyPair/ImportKeyPair to create a new SSH key pair associated with an existing EC2 instance, enabling future SSH access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker then created a SSH key pair (our initial lead alert for CreateKeyPair) for an existing EC2 instance

evidence_chunks.quote: The attacker then created a SSH key pair (our initial lead alert for CreateKeyPair) for an existing EC2 instance
The attacker then created a SSH key pair (our initial lead alert for CreateKeyPair) for an existing EC2 instance

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Attacker issued a RunInstances call to start the targeted EC2 instance, which was not running at the time.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This instance was not running at the time so the attacker turned it on via a RunInstances call

evidence_chunks.quote: This instance was not running at the time so the attacker turned it on via a RunInstances call
This instance was not running at the time so the attacker turned it on via a RunInstances call

attack_chain.description: Using the newly backdoored security group ingress and the attacker-created SSH key pair, the adversary gained command-line (SSH) access to the targeted EC2 instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Ultimately, this series of actions resulted in command line access to the targeted EC2 instance

evidence_chunks.quote: Ultimately, this series of actions resulted in command line access to the targeted EC2 instance
Ultimately, this series of actions resulted in command line access to the targeted EC2 instance

initial_access_vector: Use of 8 compromised IAM user access keys (suspected leaked via public repository/exposed database or lifted from developer machines) authenticated via AWS CLI from suspicious IPs without MFA.
persistence_mechanism: Creation of a new EC2 SSH key pair (CreateKeyPair) associated with an existing instance, plus ongoing use of multiple valid IAM access keys (redundant access).
privilege_escalation: No true privilege escalation; attacker leveraged an over-permissioned IAM user key with broad EC2 permissions (Valid Accounts).
